terms.definition: Behaving badly or not obeying rules, especially in a playful way. The boy in the poem is naughty because he runs away without permission.
terms.exampleUsage: The naughty boy ran away from home to explore Scotland.

terms.definition: A doll or figure that can be moved by pulling strings, using hands, or other means. Pinocchio is a special puppet made of wood.
terms.exampleUsage: Geppetto carved a beautiful puppet out of wood.

terms.definition: A person who makes and repairs things made of wood, like furniture, doors, and shelves. Geppetto was a carpenter who made Pinocchio.
terms.exampleUsage: The carpenter made a wooden chair for the classroom.

terms.definition: The marks used in writing to separate sentences and make meaning clear. Full stops, commas, question marks, and exclamation marks are all punctuation marks.
terms.exampleUsage: Good punctuation makes your writing easy to read and understand.

terms.definition: Wanting to learn or know more about something. The naughty boy was curious about what Scotland looked like.
terms.exampleUsage: The curious boy wanted to find out what was inside the old box.

terms.definition: Telling the truth and not lying or cheating. Pinocchio had to learn to be honest because his nose grew when he lied.
terms.exampleUsage: An honest person always tells the truth, even when it is difficult.

terms.definition: A sudden cry or remark expressing surprise, joy, pain, or excitement. An exclamation mark (!) is used at the end of such sentences.
terms.exampleUsage: 'What a surprise!' is an exclamation showing strong feeling.

terms.definition: Liking to play tricks and have fun in a slightly naughty way. It is similar to naughty but often in a harmless, playful manner.
terms.exampleUsage: The mischievous kitten knocked over the flower vase.

terms.definition: An exciting and sometimes risky experience or journey. Pinocchio goes on many adventures before becoming a real boy.
terms.exampleUsage: Pinocchio had many adventures before he learned his lessons.

terms.definition: Something that happens as a result of an action. When Pinocchio lies, the consequence is that his nose grows longer.
terms.exampleUsage: The consequence of not studying is doing poorly on the test.
